description: Fix the systemic rc-dock stale closure bug. Every callback and value prop passed through MatslopDockLayout's loadTab cache is frozen at mount time. The fix: move ALL dynamic value props into AppContext, and stabilize ALL callbacks via refs so they have [] deps.

userStories.title: Stabilize broken callbacks in App.tsx via ref pattern
userStories.description:
Six callbacks in App.tsx have state variables in their dependency arrays, making them unstable. When rc-dock caches the React element, the stale closure captures old state.

Fix each one by reading from a ref instead of closing over state:

1. `runCaptureAndRefresh` depends on `octaveStatus.engineStatus` in its dep array. Fix: read `octaveEngineStatusRef.current` (already exists) inside the callback instead. Remove octaveStatus.engineStatus from deps, make deps `[]`.

2. `handleRunScript` depends on `[runCaptureAndRefresh]`. Once #1 is stable ([] deps), handleRunScript becomes stable too since its only dep is now stable. Verify it ends up with `[]` or only stable deps.

3. `handleRunSection` depends on `[runCaptureAndRefresh]`. Same cascade as #2.

4. `handleCommandExecuted` — check its deps. If it depends on runCaptureAndRefresh, same cascade.

5. `handleCallStackSelect` depends on `[callStack]`. Fix: create `const callStackRef = useRef(callStack)` and sync it via `callStackRef.current = callStack` after the useState. Read `callStackRef.current[index]` inside the callback. Change deps to `[]`.

6. `refreshAllWatches` depends on `[evaluateWatch, octaveStatus.engineStatus]`. Fix: read `octaveEngineStatusRef.current` inside, and create/use `watchesRef` for the watches list. `evaluateWatch` is already stable.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT change any component interfaces or props. Only change callback implementations inside App.tsx.

userStories.title: Fix EditorPanel internal stale callbacks
userStories.description:
Two callbacks inside EditorPanel.tsx have state in their deps:

1. `handleOpenFile` depends on `[tabs]`. Fix: use `tabsRef.current` (already exists at line 114) instead of `tabs`. Change deps to `[]`.

2. `handleCloseWelcome` depends on `[welcomeTabId, activeTabId]`. Fix: create `welcomeTabIdRef` synced to welcomeTabId. Use `activeTabIdRef.current` (already exists) and `welcomeTabIdRef.current` inside the callback. Change deps to `[]`.

Do NOT change any component interfaces.

userStories.title: Expand AppContext with all dynamic panel state and stable callbacks
userStories.description:
Add ALL remaining dynamic value props to AppContext so panels can read from context instead of stale rc-dock cached props.

In src/renderer/AppContext.tsx:
1. Add these value fields to AppContextValue: pendingCommand, pasteCommand, menuAction, refreshTrigger (number), debugPaused (boolean), debugFrameName (string|null), pausedLocation ({file,line}|null), editorTheme (string), editorSettings (object), callStack (array), callStackSelected (number), watches (array), figures (array), historyVersion (number), gitBadges (ReadonlyMap).

2. Add these callback fields: onRunScript, onRunSection, onCommandExecuted, onDocCommand, onMenuActionConsumed, onPasteConsumed, onHistoryChanged, onCallStackSelect, onAddWatch, onRemoveWatch, onUpdateWatch, onRefreshWatches.

3. Provide sensible defaults in createContext (null, [], 0, noop functions).

4. Import necessary types — use inline type definitions to avoid circular imports.

Then in src/renderer/App.tsx, populate ALL new fields in the `appCtx` useMemo. Use the stabilized callbacks from US-SC01. Add all value state variables to the useMemo dependency array.

IMPORTANT: Keep ALL existing AppContext fields (help*, profiler*, cwd, pendingOpenPath, etc). Only ADD new ones.
acceptanceCriteria: AppContextValue has all listed value and callback fields
createContext has defaults for every field
App.tsx appCtx useMemo populates every field

userStories.title: Update all panel components to read from AppContext
userStories.description:
Update each panel component to read dynamic state from AppContext instead of (or in addition to) props. Keep props as optional fallbacks for tests.

Pattern: `const ctx = useAppContext(); const val = ctx.field ?? propField;`

Panels to update (import useAppContext in each):

1. CommandWindow.tsx — read: pendingCommand, pasteCommand, menuAction, onCommandExecuted, onDocCommand, onMenuActionConsumed, onPasteConsumed, onHistoryChanged
2. WorkspacePanel.tsx — read: refreshTrigger, debugPaused, debugFrameName
3. EditorPanel.tsx — read: pausedLocation, editorTheme, editorSettings, menuAction, onMenuActionConsumed, onRunScript (as onRun), onRunSection
4. CallStackPanel.tsx — read: callStack (as frames), callStackSelected (as selectedIndex), onCallStackSelect (as onSelectFrame)
5. WatchesPanel.tsx — read: watches, onAddWatch, onRemoveWatch, onUpdateWatch, onRefreshWatches (as onRefresh)
6. FigurePanel.tsx — read: figures
7. CommandHistoryPanel.tsx — read: historyVersion
8. FileBrowser.tsx — read: gitBadges, cwd (as externalCwd)

For each: make the props optional (add ?) where context provides the value. Context value takes precedence (ctx.x ?? propX).

Do NOT remove props from App.tsx MatslopDockLayout slots — keep them as fallbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 panels import and use useAppContext
Each panel reads its dynamic values from context with prop fallback
Affected props are optional in interfaces
